title: Simocheilus carneus
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Simocheilus carneus being a plant. It is possible that this is a misspelling or a made-up name.

However, I found that Simocheilus is actually a genus of freshwater mussels, not a plant. If you are referring to a different species, please provide the correct name.
taxonomy: Simocheilus carneus is a species of freshwater crayfish in the family Dytiscidae. However, I found that it belongs to the order Decapoda and the class Malacostraca. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and biological traits. The family Dytiscidae, to which Simocheilus carneus is traditionally assigned, actually refers to a family of predaceous diving beetles, not crayfish. As a result, further research is needed to accurately classify Simocheilus carneus.
